description: A designed statistics/infographic card (a White House repost of WhiteHouse content, per the RT) built around a choropleth map of the United States. A two-color legend at top reads 'Net immigration fell' (light yellow) and 'Fell more than 50%' (orange); counties across the country are shaded accordingly, with most of the West and large swaths shaded orange. Black callout labels with arrows point to metro areas and percent declines: 'Seattle -31%', 'Denver metro area -72%', 'Chicago -62%', 'N.Y.C. metro area -65%', 'Washington, D.C. -44%', 'L.A. -67%', 'El Paso -95%', 'Dallas -52%', 'Miami -32%'. Below the map, in large navy type mixing bold and italic serif, is 'Net Immigration FELL In Every Metro Area in 2025', attributed to 'New York Times', with the small White House engraving at the very bottom. The technique is data-as-proof messaging crediting the administration with sharply reducing immigration nationwide, lending authority by citing the NYT. Target audience: the public and supporters, used as evidence of 'promises made, promises kept'.
notable_text: Net immigration fell / Fell more than 50% / Net Immigration FELL In Every Metro Area in 2025 / New York Times / El Paso -95%
